Metals & Mining

Your mills run at 1,000°C. Your sensors don't.

Strip mills, SAG mills, crushers, conveyors, and dewatering pumps operate in extreme heat, dust, and underground conditions. Most go unmonitored between scheduled walk-downs.

27 Failures detected early (ArcelorMittal)
800% ROI in 11 months (Nyrstar)
€650k Production loss avoided (hot strip mill)
Statistics from named customer deployments. ArcelorMittal: 27 faults detected on hot strip mill assets, January 2024 to December 2025. Nyrstar: ROI calculated on avoided downtime and emergency repair costs across zinc smelting assets.
Request a demo See the evidence
Trusted by leaders in metals and mining
ArcelorMittal thyssenkrupp Nyrstar
The problem

Every hour of downtime costs six figures

Steel mills run 24/7. Mines cannot stop dewatering. When a roller bearing fails on a hot strip mill or a borehole pump seizes 150 metres underground, the cost is measured in hours of lost production and emergency change-outs.

Hot strip mill failures

Runout table rollers, cardan shafts, and mill motors operate under extreme thermal and mechanical stress. Bearing failures on a single roller can halt the entire strip line. In automotive steel, one hour of downtime idles downstream assembly plants within hours, costing $2M+.

Underground dewatering pumps

Borehole pumps sit 150 to 180 metres underground. Pulling one for inspection costs thousands, takes days, and requires heavy lifting equipment. While pulled, groundwater rises. Most sites run pumps to failure because there is no practical way to inspect them in situ.

Conveyor and crusher availability

Belt conveyors move ROM material from pit to plant. A single conveyor failure stops the entire material flow chain. Belt misalignment is the leading downtime cause. Crushers experience extreme shock loading that destroys most sensor installations.

Extreme operating environments

Sensor failure rates reach 30% in harsh conditions. Temperatures exceed 1,000°C at the strip mill face. Dust, moisture, and vibration degrade instrumentation within months. The 52% "dark asset" gap in metals and mining is the second-highest of any sector.

Why SAM4

Monitor from the MCC. Not the mill floor.

No sensor survives 1,000°C at the strip mill face. But the motor current at the MCC carries the signature of mechanical degradation in the driven equipment. Electrical Signature Analysis (ESA) bypasses the access problem entirely.

No sensors on the asset

SAM4 monitors from the motor control cabinet. No equipment on the mill floor, in the crusher housing, or underground. No ATEX certification at the measurement point. No sensor change-outs in extreme heat or dust.

Reads through harsh environments

Heat destroys sensors. Dust blinds cameras. Vibration shakes accelerometers loose. ESA reads the electrical signature at the MCC, where conditions are controlled. The motor current carries mechanical fault signatures regardless of what happens at the asset.

Covers the full drivetrain

One measurement point per asset monitors motor, coupling, gearbox, and driven equipment simultaneously. SAM4 detects bearing wear, misalignment, imbalance, belt degradation, and electrical faults across crushers, mills, conveyors, pumps, and fans.

Deploys without shutdown

Clip-on current transformers install in the MCC in under 30 minutes. No production interruption. No confined-space entry. No crane hire for underground access. Scale from pilot to fleet without touching the process.

What we monitor

Critical assets in metals and mining

SAM4 monitors any AC motor-driven rotating equipment from the motor control cabinet.

Crushers & mills

Ball mills, SAG mills, jaw crushers, and cone crushers. Bearing wear, gearbox degradation, and motor faults detected across the full comminution circuit.

Crusher monitoring details →

Conveyors

Belt conveyors and material handling drives. Belt degradation, misalignment, bearing failure, and drive system faults on ROM and product conveyors.

Conveyor monitoring details →

Pumps

Dewatering pumps, slurry pumps, and process water pumps. Cavitation, seal wear, bearing degradation, and efficiency loss. Includes borehole pumps 150m+ underground.

Pump monitoring details →

Fans & blowers

Ventilation fans, process blowers, and cooling fans. Imbalance, bearing wear, and belt degradation on surface and underground ventilation systems.

Fan monitoring details →

MV motors

Medium-voltage mill drives, crusher motors, and large conveyor drives. Stator winding faults, rotor bar defects, and supply-side anomalies on motors from 1 MW to 15 MW+.

MV motor monitoring details →
Proof

Named customers. Quantified outcomes.

Featured

ArcelorMittal: 27 failures detected, 31 hours of downtime prevented

SAM4 monitored ArcelorMittal's hot strip mill assets and detected 27 developing faults before any caused unplanned downtime. Detection covered bearing wear on runout table rollers, coupling degradation on cardan shafts, and electrical faults on mill motors.

27
Failures detected early
31 hrs
Unplanned downtime prevented
€650k
Production loss avoided
Read case study →

800%

Nyrstar

The zinc smelter deployed SAM4 across critical rotating assets and achieved 800% return on investment within 11 months. Avoided downtime and emergency repair costs drove the payback.

Read case study →

16

ArcelorMittal (hot strip mill)

SAM4 detected degrading bearings on 16 runout table rollers before any failed. Planned change-outs during scheduled maintenance windows prevented extended production shutdowns.

Read case study →
All outcomes independently confirmed through maintenance work orders and post-intervention inspection. ArcelorMittal data covers January 2024 to December 2025. Nyrstar ROI calculated on documented avoided costs over 11-month deployment period.

Ready to see what SAM4 detects on your assets?

Start your asset audit →
Installation

No sensors on the mill floor

1

Clip on at the MCC

Current transformers clip around the phase wires inside the motor control cabinet. Non-intrusive. No production shutdown required. Takes under 30 minutes per asset.

2

Connect via cellular gateway

Data transmits over 4G/5G. No IT integration needed for deployment. Store-and-forward buffering handles connectivity gaps in remote mine sites.

3

Monitor from anywhere

The SAM4 dashboard shows asset health, fault alerts, and energy performance. Integrates with existing SCADA and CMMS via OPC-UA or REST API.

Installation photo
FAQ

Common questions from metals and mining engineers

  • ESA measures from the motor control cabinet, not the asset. The MCC operates at ambient temperature regardless of conditions at the mill face. Motor current carries the signature of mechanical degradation in the driven equipment, including rollers operating at 1,000°C+. No sensors are exposed to extreme heat.
  • Yes. VFDs change output frequency, which complicates traditional vibration analysis. SAM4 normalises baselines using VFD speed and load data, isolating mechanical faults from drive-induced harmonics. SAM4 monitors both DOL and VFD-driven assets across crushers, conveyors, and mill drives.
  • There is no depth limit. ESA monitors from the MCC on the surface. The electrical connection between the MCC and the motor carries fault signatures regardless of cable length. Samotics monitors borehole pumps at 150 to 200 metres at mining sites.
  • Vibration sensors on conveyors suffer from environmental noise, dust ingress, and physical damage from material handling. ESA is immune to these because the measurement happens at the MCC. SAM4 detects belt degradation, bearing wear, and drive faults on conveyors without any sensors in the material flow path.
  • Installation takes under 30 minutes per asset. No production shutdown required. A typical pilot of 20 to 30 assets deploys in one to two days. Fleet rollouts scale linearly. Cellular connectivity means no IT infrastructure changes for initial deployment.
  • SAM4 monitors crushers, mills, conveyors, pumps, fans, and MV motors across metals and mining operations. ArcelorMittal and Nyrstar are named reference customers. Detection coverage and limitations are documented per drive type and operating regime.
Get started

Monitor every asset. No sensors on the mill floor.

Start with your highest-risk assets. See results within weeks. Scale from there.

Monitoring the assets your sensors can't reach.
